title: leontodon dubius
intro: Leontodon dubius, also known as the lesser hawk's-beard or dune hawk's-beard, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, Leontodon dubius has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The plant is said to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and alleviating respiratory problems. Leontodon dubius is often prepared as a decoction or infusion for internal use, and its leaves and flowers can be used in herbal teas, salves, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
